title: Double-Fired Korean Garlic Binchotan-Grilled Bokkeum
description: A rich and savory Busan-style dish featuring double-fried garlic cloves grilled to perfection over a Binchotan grill. Best served as a late-night anju.

ingredients.name: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality, aged soy sauce

instructions: Heat the Binchotan charcoal in a charcoal chimney until ash has formed.
Transfer the charcoal to a Binchotan grill and preheat to 500°F (260°C).
In a small bowl, mix together gochujang, gochugaru, and sesame oil.
Double-fry the garlic cloves in a skillet over medium heat until crispy, then set aside.
In the same skillet, add the soy sauce, black pepper, and thinly sliced ginger. Cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the green onions to the skillet and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until slightly wilted.
Transfer the garlic cloves to the preheated Binchotan grill and grill for 2-3 minutes per side, until caramelized.
Brush the gochujang-gochugaru mixture onto the garlic cloves during the last minute of grilling.
Serve the grilled garlic cloves hot, garnished with crushed pork rinds if desired.
Optional: serve with a side of steamed rice or noodles to soak up the savory sauce.
Garnish with additional green onions and thinly sliced ginger, if desired.
